strategic_profile.executive_summary: NJIT is a premier public polytechnic research university offering high-ROI STEM education in the NYC metropolitan area. With R1 research status and a highly diverse student body, it emphasizes practical, career-focused learning and extensive industry co-op opportunities.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic but highly Numbers-focused (Rigor and Academic GPA are heavily weighted)

analyst_takeaways.insight: Acceptance rates hover around 65-69%, making it moderately selective, though its Honors College is highly competitive with a ~6.8% acceptance rate.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Strong STEM students use NJIT as a solid target/safety, while high achievers target the lucrative Honors College.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median 10-year earnings reach $91,874, placing NJIT among the top public schools nationwide for economic mobility and ROI.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Graduates experience excellent career outcomes, particularly in engineering and computer science.

analyst_takeaways.insight: NJIT is heavily commuter-based but boasts significant diversity and a highly academically oriented student body.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Campus life can be quieter on weekends, requiring students to actively seek out clubs, networking, and social events.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Diverse, academically intense, and primarily commuter-driven, though on-campus residential life is expanding through new apartment-style dorms.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in urban Newark with immediate access to mass transit, putting New York City a 20-minute train ride away for internships and networking.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 Doctoral University with robust undergraduate research opportunities in makerspaces, cybersecurity, and applied engineering.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional post-COVID-19; however, SAT/ACT scores are strongly recommended for merit scholarship consideration and required for certain accelerated programs.

gpa.notes: Expected to have a mix of As and Bs, predominantly As. Math/Science rigor is heavily emphasized.

colleges.name: Albert Dorman Honors College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: High-achieving applicants with top-tier SATs (1475+) and leadership profiles seeking merit scholarships and priority registration.
colleges.housing_profile: Honors-specific housing available; robust residential learning communities.
colleges.student_archetype: Ambitious researchers, future medical/law students via accelerated tracks.

colleges.name: Ying Wu College of Computing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students targeting software engineering, cybersecurity, and data science.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.notes: Students are expected to earn a grade of B or better in CS 100 and a C or better in all other CS courses.

application_deadlines.notes: Early Action I and II available.
Rolling admissions heavily utilized after RD deadlines.

supplemental_requirements.details: Portfolio of creative work required for College of Architecture and Design applicants.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Strong emphasis on quantitative preparation; applicants to STEM fields must display high math proficiency.

major_selection_tactics: Apply Early Action, as it accounts for over 60% of admitted students.
Showcase specific computing or engineering project work in the essay to align with NJIT's polytechnic focus.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Undeclared/Exploratory pathways are available and common for freshmen who meet general university admissions criteria but are undecided on a specific engineering/tech track.

scholarships.name: Albert Dorman Honors College Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: 100% of Honors students receive merit scholarship packages.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 3, 4, or 5 generally accepted, though higher scores (4/5) are recommended for specific rigorous STEM courses.
ap_policy.usage: Used for course equivalency and general education credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Must earn at least 33 upper-division credits at NJIT.

what_it_takes: Strong high school GPA heavily skewed toward As
Genuine interest in technology, engineering, or applied sciences
essay_tips: Demonstrate interest in NJIT's specific resources (e.g., Makerspace)
red_flags: Low math grades or lack of quantitative rigor
insights: Social life requires proactive effort due to the large commuter population.
Academics are considered 'Ivy League level' in difficulty, particularly introductory math courses.
